memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir George "Malcolm"  Campbell XIVth of Loudoun
memoryArtifacts.description:
Sir George Campbell of Loudoun was born circa 1449 at Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. He was the son of George Campbell of Loudoun and Elizabeth Stewart.[1][2][3]

zh.Eglinton, Ayrshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾格林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當George Campbell於大約1449年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度時，他的父親George Campbell 13th of Loudoun30歲，母親Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley23歲。他於1484年與Marion Auchinleck結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他在蘇格蘭艾斯爾被列為sheriff of ayrshire。他去世於1491年3月9日在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾享年43歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾蒙。

zh-hans.Eglinton, Ayrshire, Scotland: 苏格兰艾斯爾艾格林頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Campbell于大约1449年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度时，他的父亲George Campbell 13th of Loudoun30岁，母亲Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley23岁。他于1484年与Marion Auchinleck结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他在苏格兰艾斯爾被列为sheriff of ayrshire。他去世于1491年3月9日在英国苏格兰艾斯爾享年43岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾蒙。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When George Campbell was born about 1449, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, George Campbell 13th of Loudoun, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, was 23. He married Marion Auchinleck in 1484. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as sheriff of ayrshire in Ayrshire, Scotland. He died on 9 March 1491, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Campbell est né environ 1449, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, George Campbell 13th of Loudoun, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Marion Auchinleck en 1484. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of ayrshire à Ayrshire, Écosse. Il est décédé le 9 mars 1491, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Kilmun, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Campbell um 1449 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Campbell 13th of Loudoun 30 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley 23. Er hat 1484 Marion Auchinleck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff of ayrshire in Ayrshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 9. März 1491 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Campbell è nato il circa 1449 nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, George Campbell 13th of Loudoun, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Marion Auchinleck nel 1484. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of ayrshire nel Ayrshire, Scotland. Morì il 9 marzo 1491, nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: George Campbellが約1449年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンで生まれた時，父George Campbell 13th of Loudounは30歳，母Elizabeth Stewart of Darnleyは23歳であった。1484年にMarion Auchinleckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。スコットランドエイルシュアイアでの職業はsheriff of ayrshireと記録されている。彼は1491年3月9日にイギリススコットランドエイルシュアイアで43歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルキルムンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: George Campbell(이)가 대략 1449년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Campbell 13th of Loudoun(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1484년 Marion Auchinleck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 sheriff of ayrshire이었다. 그는 1491년 3월 9일 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬먼에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Campbell nasceu aproximadamente 1449, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, George Campbell 13th of Loudoun, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Marion Auchinleck em 1484. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff of ayrshire em Ayrshire, Scotland. Ele faleceu em 9 de março de 1491, em Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Campbell родился около 1449 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени George Campbell 13th of Loudoun было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Marion Auchinleck в 1484 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии sheriff of ayrshire в местности аиршир, Шотландия. Он умер 9 марта 1491 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности килмун, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Campbell nació aproximadamente 1449, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, George Campbell 13th of Loudoun, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Marion Auchinleck en 1484. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff of ayrshire en Ayrshire, Scotland. Murió el 9 de marzo de 1491, en Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland.
